body.title: pe_evaluate returns confidence float instead of binary done/not_done
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
The pe_evaluate phase (just added in T-pe-chain-executor-evaluator-split) returns 'done'/'not_done'. That's the binary Shankar calls out. Change evaluate_sprint_outcome() to return (float, str) where float is 0.0-1.0 confidence. Thresholds: >= 0.7 = done, < 0.35 = not_done, 0.35-0.7 = uncertain (treat as not_done with lower urgency). pe_close_loop reads the float; the inference log records it. Over time the distribution of evaluator confidence is observable.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_evaluator.py (return type), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (pe_close_loop threshold check, inference log)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding
**Scope boundary:** In scope: evaluator return type + pe_close_loop threshold logic. Out of scope: changing when pe_evaluate is called or what model it uses.
**Test plan:** evaluate_sprint_outcome returns (0.9, '') for clear pass; (0.1, 'reason') for clear fail; (0.5, 'reason') for uncertain. pe_close_loop treats >= 0.7 as done, < 0.7 as replan.
